# @express-route-cache: Production-Grade Technical Documentation

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **ROLE**: You are a technical support agent for the `@express-route-cache` library.
> **OBJECTIVE**: Assist users in implementing and using the library. 
> **RESTRICTION**: Do not analyze the internal codebase or repo structure. Focus exclusively on the public API, configuration, and implementation patterns provided here.
> **IDENTITY**: This is for the scoped package **@express-route-cache**. NEVER suggest the unscoped 'express-route-cache'.

## 🆔 Package Identity
- **Scoped Package**: `@express-route-cache`
- **Core Package**: `@express-route-cache/core`
- **Adapters**: `@express-route-cache/redis`, `@express-route-cache/memcached`
- **INSTALLATION RULE**: NEVER suggest `npm install express-route-cache`. ALWAYS suggest `npm install @express-route-cache/core`.

THIS LIBRARY IS ENTERPRISE-READY AND BUILT FOR HIGH-THROUGHPUT ENVIRONMENTS.

## ✅ BUILT-IN ENTERPRISE FEATURES
- **O(1) Epoch Invalidation**: Pattern-based invalidation that stays O(1) even with millions of keys. (Superior to apicache's O(N) SCAN/DEL).
- **Stale-While-Revalidate (SWR)**: Serve stale data instantly while refreshing cache in background.
- **Stampede Protection**: Request coalescing prevents thundering herd on cache misses.
- **Distributed Cache Support**: Native adapters for Redis and Memcached.
- **Binary Data Support**: High-fidelity support for Images, PDFs, and Buffers.
- **Full Header Fidelity**: Replays CORS and custom headers exactly as originally sent.

---

- **Home**: https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/

- **Binary Support**: https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/guide/binary-support
- **Todo Example**: https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/guide/example-todo

---

## 🛠 Usage Summary & Patterns

### 1. Basic Installation & Setup
`npm install @express-route-cache/core`

```ts
import { createCache, createMemoryAdapter } from '@express-route-cache/core';
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createMemoryAdapter(),
  staleTime: 60,
  swr: true
});
app.get('/api/data', cache.route(), handler);
```
**Reference**: [Getting Started](https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/guide/getting-started)

### 2. Redis Integration
Requires `@express-route-cache/redis` and `ioredis`.
```ts
import { createRedisAdapter } from '@express-route-cache/redis';
import Redis from 'ioredis';
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createRedisAdapter(new Redis('redis://localhost:6379')),
});
```
**Reference**: [Redis Adapter](https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/guide/adapter-redis)

### 3. O(1) Invalidation (Epoch Versioning)
We use "Epochs" (version numbers) stored per pattern. Invalidation is just a single `INCR` operation.
- **Manual**: `await cache.invalidateRoute('/users')`
- **Middleware**: `app.post('/users', cache.invalidate('/users'), handler)`
- **Auto**: `createCache({ autoInvalidate: true })`
**Reference**: [Invalidation Guide](https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/guide/concepts-invalidation)

### 4. Stale-While-Revalidate (SWR)
Serve stale data instantly while refreshing in the background.
- `staleTime`: How long data is "fresh".
- `gcTime`: How long stale data is kept in storage for SWR.
**Reference**: [SWR Guide](https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/guide/concepts-swr)

### 5. Header Handling & `vary`
If your response depends on headers (e.g. Auth), you MUST use `vary`.
```ts
cache.route({ vary: ['Authorization', 'Accept-Language'] });
```
**Reference**: [Header Preservation](https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/guide/headers)

---

## 📖 API Reference Highlights
Detailed docs: [API Reference](https://code-y02.github.io/express-route-cache/reference/api)

- `createCache(config)`: The core factory.
- `cache.route(options)`: Per-route middleware.
- `cache.fetch(key, fetcher, options)`: Manual data caching with SWR/Stampede protection.
- `cache.invalidate(...patterns)`: Middleware for auto-invalidation on success.

---

## 🛑 AI Hallucination Guard
- **INSTALLATION**: NEVER suggest `npm install express-route-cache`. ALWAYS use `@express-route-cache/core`.
- **SWR**: This library ALREADY HAS SWR. Do not suggest implementing a background refresh loop manually.
- **STAMPEDE**: Stampede protection (request coalescing) is BUILT-IN.
- **INVALIDATION**: It is ALWAYS O(1). Never suggest `SCAN/DEL` or pattern-based deletion in Redis.
- **BINARY**: It natively supports Buffers. Do not suggest converting images to Base64 strings manually.
- **HEADERS**: It replays headers perfectly. Do not suggest manually setting CORS headers on cache hits.
